entry.title: How to get Audit Trail Section in Print Reports in SAP Concur
entry.content:
I always look up the internet for ideas and solutions whenever I get stuck with something and finding what we are looking for can become a tedious task especially in niche technologies like Concur. And when I don't find the exact thing but later come up with a solution, I tend to make a playbook so that it might come in handy in future. But now that I have got an idea how blogging works, I feel this is even better as it reaches a wider audience and can help someone like me who is finding a solution.

So coming to the task that made me sweep the various documents and posts was a very valid ask from one of our business team members. They wanted to have the Audit Trail section come in the Print Report so that they can download it.

The business case as I understood from him was many times the admin team members who do the initial audit get blamed for Late fees, which happens when the report is not processed on time.

The Audit team find it difficult to get into the audit trail section for those reports which are in question take a screenshot of the day and time they have approved it from their to ensure it was not them who were the reason for late fees but the submission from users were found to be delayed.

This cumbersome manual process is something we can eliminate if we can have a Print Report which gives out the Audit Trail section. They can just download the PDF and send it to the respective stakeholders.

So when trying to check out the existing templates we do not have any with regards to Audit trail. And when you go to the internet and browse there is just one similar question back from 2022 which was never answered.

And then when you check the training documents from concur only the below portion is mentioned 

  • Audit Trail: Displays audit trail data, including confirmation agreement acceptance records, to the printed report. This data appears in a non-configurable preset format.

Displays audit trail data, including confirmation agreement acceptance records, to the printed report. This data appears in a non-configurable preset format.

Syntax: $auditdata_XXXX

(where XXXX is the content section name, such as $auditdata_AuditTrail)

Also to note is The printed report will create the audit trail information based on the role of the user who is printing the report. For example, a processor sees more types of information on the audit trail than a submitter. When the processor prints the report, their audit trail section will contain more details than when the submitter prints the same report.

But apart from that there is no real information about anything. So I have come up with the HTML Code that will ease up the process and also create a template which will include only the Audit Trail section.

The Actual Code:

<tr>
<td>
<table class="cStdForm"
cellspacing="0"
style="width: 100%"
cellpadding="0"
border="0"
summary="$!ui.localize('table.summary.printreportreceipt')">
<tr><td> $auditdata_AuditTrailSection </td></tr>
</table>
</td>
</tr>

Now that we have the code it is just a matter of fitting it into a template and assigning that Print report to the required policy.

Step 1:

Go to Home->Expense->Printed Reports->Print Templates

Here copy an existing template and rename it . Then open the newly copied report and in the template code make sure to place our code which is given above in a place where you desire. The position can be seen by clicking on the preview layout. Then click on save.

Make sure you are careful with all the open and close tags when you remove contents you don't need from the template. 

Step 2:

Now move to the Print Formats tab. Similar way copy an existing Format and rename it. In the Print 

template field choose the template we just created in the previous step.

and preview the layout and make sure it is as per the requirements.

Now go back to the Print Format tab and click on Modify content

In the Modify Content page you will see all the headers of the sections we have inserted in the template.

Now click on the Audit Trail and click on Modify content.

In the modify content of the Audit trail section give a suitable header and also make sure you check the Print Confirmation Agreement Audit trail records check box.

Step 3:

Now head to the policy where this Print Report is required and enable it at Policy level.

Once this is done the go to the processor, search for the report for which we need the Audit Trail to be printed out and download the same.

The final output of the PDF look like below with the Employee data and the Audit trail section.

So there it is , a way to add the Audit Trail section to the Print Template and I hope this will help anyone who are looking for a solution to this type of a requirement.

Thanks a lot everyone!

entry.title: Integration & Exception Monitoring Two-Tier ERP
entry.content:
Introduction

Integration and exception monitoring in a Two-Tier ERP system are essential to ensure seamless data flow and consistency between Headquarter and Subsidiary systems. They help maintain operational efficiency, avoid data discrepancies, and support accurate reporting across business units. Real-time exception monitoring enables quick identification and resolution of issues, preventing disruptions. Proper monitoring ensures that any deviations are swiftly addressed, ensuring smooth, unified business operations

Set up:

Let see a detailed view on how we can set up Integration & Exception Monitoring for SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Public cloud Edition and CI.

The details are also available in reference Link.

Process

Let’s deep dive into the process looking at the use case.

Case 1:  In 2EL scenario  we send the sales order to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition  from On-premise(SAP S/4HANA) system as Headquarter wants to purchase from Subsidiary.

In this case the communication is failing at Headquarter.

Case 2:  In 2EL scenario we send the sales order to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition  from On-premise system(SAP S/4HANA) as Headquarter wants to purchase from Subsidiary.

In this case the communication is failing at CI which is a middleware.

References

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/landscape-setup

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/setup-managed-services.html

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations.html?anchorId=section_1159650512

entry.title: ABAP Object Oriented Programming (OOP): Builder pattern Cloud Ready Version.
entry.content:
Builder Pattern Cloud Ready Implementation

All code can be found in my GitHub repository: https://github.com/IlyaPrusakou/ABAPBuilderPattern

In this repository you can find implementation of Builder Pattern in ABAP. The main point is the realization of pattern made on special version of ABAP language - ABAP for Cloud Development. So that there is 100% cloud compatibility.

Just copy and use it.

1. Introduction.

The Builder pattern is one of the classic creational design pattern. The main idea of the one is to separate process of creation of Product class (Product, Car, Car Schema and etc.) A logic for creation of Product class is handled into Builder class.

Below you can find Pattern Schemas and Implementation Details, you can skip theoretical notes and go to these sections.

2. Theoretical notes.

2.1. Types of the Builder pattern

There are 3 types of builder pattern I've differentiated. Each of them can be found correspondingly in GitHub files: src/zpru_bld1, src/zpru_bld2, src/zpru_bld3.

2.1.1. Simple Builder:

2.1.2. Builder with Director

2.1.3. Builder with Director and Interfaces

2.2. Semantical structure of pattern

All classes and interfaces can play roles of:

  1. Director
  2. Builder
  3. Product.

Regarding of Product role I would like to underline distinction of at least two type of specialization:

2.2.1. Factual specialization

Factual specialization means that all instances of product has the same class or interface, but they differ by data preserved into. The example:

As you can see there are two instances of class Car, but they are varied with qualified field model, which can be read-only field, set inside constructor.

2.2.2. Type specialization

As you can guessed that type of specialization is based on usage of not only the same class with different values of qualified fields, but also different classes or interfaces. Moreover, there is room for amplification of interfaces raised. The same example:

Regarding Builder role I would elucidate method Build. The method creates an instance of Product and set all data, preserved in Builder instance, into product instance. We can set up builder once with setters methods. All the data will be plugged in Product instance. Each invocation of Build creates new instance of Product with data from Builder. The Builder is a source of data, along with that Product is a recipient of data. Moreover, there is space for implementation of specialization of product. For instance, Builder can have multiple Build methods (buildProductA, buildProductB and etc.), returning different instance setup or different class types.

Director role is just additional stage of Product creation. When Builder is a distinct layer for creation of Products, but Director is a layer to create or setup of Builders. Hence the question of specialization rises again there. For example, we can make multiple methods (setupBuilderA, setupBuilderB and etc.) for setup of different Builders.

3. Pattern Schemas.

3.1. Simple Builder

3.2. Builder with Director.

3.3. Builder With Director and Interfaces

 

4. Implementation Details

4.1. Simple Builders

  1. Car class has private constructor an establish friendship with Car Builder class.
  2. Car class has only getters methods to receive data from.
  3. Car class has constructor taking all data to setup it as class attributes.
  4. Builder contains only setter methods to setup builder data as class attributes.
  5. Builder has build method, returning instance of Car class.
  6. Builder's build method passes all data from Builder instance into constructor of Car class.

4.2. Simple Builders Usage pattern.

 

    " Create mercedes builder
    " pass mandatory fields
    DATA(lo_mercedes_builder) = NEW zpru_cl_bld1_car_builder( iv_id    = '1'
                                                              iv_brand = 'MERCEDES'
                                                              iv_model = 'C 180 T-Model' ).
    " pass optional fields into builder
    lo_mercedes_builder->set_price('100000' )->set_multimedia( 'AVANTGARDE' )->set_equipment( 'Memory Package' )->set_assistance_system('Driving Assistance Package Plus' ).

    " construct final object of mercedes
    DATA(lo_mercedes_c_180) = lo_mercedes_builder->build( ).

 

4.3. Builder with Director

  1. 1 - 6 points for Simple Builder are true
  2. Director class has specialized methods to setup different Builders, e.g. buildMercedes, buildPorcshe and etc.
  3. Director's specialized methods has importing parameter of type of Builder class(Builder Interface) to inject Builder instance
  4. Director's methods provide distinguished setup for different Builders' instances.

4.4. Builder with Director Usage pattern

 

    DATA(lo_director) = NEW zpru_cl_bld2_car_director( ).
    DATA(lo_mercedes_builder) = NEW zpru_cl_bld2_car_builder( iv_brand = 'MERCEDES'
                                                              iv_model = 'C 180 T-Model' ).
    lo_director->build_mercedes( io_builder = lo_mercedes_builder ).

    lo_mercedes_builder->set_color( 'BLUE' ).
    lo_mercedes_builder->set_id( '1' ).
    DATA(lo_mercedes_c180_1_blue) = lo_mercedes_builder->build( ).

 

4.5. Builder With Director and Interfaces

  1. Basically, 1 - 6 points for Simple Builder are true.
  2. Create common interface for all Product class(Car class and Car Schema class).
  3. Create product specialized interfaces for Car entity and Car Schema entity.
  4. Create common interface for Builder classes(Car Builder class and Car Schema class).
  5. Create Builder specialized interfaces for Car Builder entity and Car Schema Builder entity.
  6. Rework friendship inside Car and Car Schema classes, make it points onto Builder common interface.
  7. Use type of product specialized interface as returning type of Builder's build methods.
  8. Use type of builder specialized interface as importing type of Director's build methods.

4.6. Builder With Director and Interfaces Usage pattern.

 

    DATA lo_mercedes_builder_car TYPE REF TO zpru_if_bld3_builder_car.
    DATA lo_mercedes_builder_schema TYPE REF TO zpru_if_bld3_builder_schema.
    DATA lo_director TYPE REF TO zpru_cl_bld3_car_director.

    lo_director = NEW zpru_cl_bld3_car_director( ).
    lo_mercedes_builder_car = NEW zpru_cl_bld3_car_builder( iv_brand = 'MERCEDES'
                                                            iv_model = 'C 180 T-Model' ).
    lo_director->build_mercedes_car( io_builder = lo_mercedes_builder_car ).

    lo_mercedes_builder_car->zpru_if_bld3_builder~set_id( '1' ).
    DATA(lo_mercedes_c180_car) = lo_mercedes_builder_car->build( ).

    lo_mercedes_builder_schema = NEW zpru_cl_bld3_schema_builder( iv_brand = 'MERCEDES'
                                                                  iv_model = 'C 180 T-Model' ).

    lo_director->build_mercedes_schema( io_builder = lo_mercedes_builder_schema ).
    lo_mercedes_builder_schema->zpru_if_bld3_builder~set_id( '2' ).
    DATA(lo_mercedes_c180_schema) = lo_mercedes_builder_schema->build( ).

entry.title: Material Staging in Production Supply Area-WM/PP Integration
entry.content:
Integrating warehouse and production processes through material staging at the production supply area. This integration will ensure a seamless flow of materials, reduce downtime, and improve overall productivity.

Configuration Process

Storage type for Production supply area (123-Prod. Supply Area)

SPRO -> Logistics Execution -> Warehouse Management -> Interfaces -> Define Production

Click Maintain

Click Assign (319-Mvt. Type Replenishment)

Click PP

Maintain wm request as “X-Create TR upon order release” Under Transport sub screen

Click Warehouse Number

Tick PP Interface Active

Note: We can proceed without checking this indicator also, process flow works as follows;

If checked

Prod. Order -> Transfer stock to Prod. Supply area -> TR -> TO -> Confirm TO -> Goods Issue to Prod. Order

If not checked

Prod. Order -> Goods Issue to Prod. Order -> TR -> TO -> Confirm TO

Test Scenario:

Step 1: Create finished material and components, make sure you have stock for the component material.

(Extend WM, work scheduling, MRP views for materials)

Step 2: Assign Prod. Supply area to work center using transaction CR02

Step 3: Create Control cycle using transaction LPK1

Staging Indicator 1 represents “Items picked against respective production order only”.

Fixed bin created & assigned for prod. Supply area storage type(123) i.e wm-pp-area

Step 4: Create Production Order using transaction CO01

The below component copied from the BOM,

To produce 1 PC of final product we require 25 PC of raw material (wm-pp-raw-mat)

Click on Operations

Work center operation assigned here

Click Release order and save the Order

Step 5: Stage the material with respect to production order in Prod. Supply Area using transaction LP10

Enter Requested Qty and save

Step 6: Check the Open transfer Requirements in transaction LB10

Step 7: Create and confirm TO

Step 8: Check the stock either in LS26 or LX02

As per production requirement 25 PC stagged in Prod. Supply area

Step 9: Goods Issue Against Production order using transaction MIGO

Once material document posted stock automatically consumed to the production form prod. Supply area

Step 10: Check the stock

Available stock 25 PC and 25 PC from prod. Supply area storage type got consumed against the production order.

entry.title: Flexible Item Category Determination in SAP Cloud Public - Custom Adaptions
entry.content:
Why Flexible Item Category Determination Matters!

As Sales Configuration Experts, we all know that the Item categories control how sales document items behave and thus meet different demands in the business processes.

However, the limitations of the Sales Public Cloud prevent the creation of Z-Sales Document Types. As a result, we are unable to control the determination of the appropriate item category that will control the item behavior in the sales documents. Consequently, most users are required to make manual changes when processing these documents.

Flexible Determination

Using the Flexible Item Category Determination, The system can evaluate specific business conditions and propose an item category according to your business rules instead of the Default Item Category assignment.

Example:

Your client needs to manage the behavior of business processes by selecting the appropriate Order Reason. For a certain type of orders, a down payment is required for all products. Therefore, the client will select "Down Payment" from the dropdown list. Once this selection is made, the system can propose the CBAO item category when specific business conditions outlined in a decision table are met, allowing the billing plan to be automatically enabled.

Define the following Business Rule to Your Decision Table.

 

Sales Document Type Item Category Group Item Category Usage Item Category of Higher-Level Item SD Order Reason Item Category

 

= OR= NORM<empty><empty>= 01 (Down Payment)= CBAO

Note: If the user doesn't select any order reason or any available alternatives, the system finds no logic in your decision table. The system proposes the default item category TAN.

Step-by-Step Guide to Configuration

Step 1: Create PRFplus Objects

you can create Business Rule Framework Plus (BRFplus) applications, functions, and expressions from templates.

You perform this in the configuration activity Create BRFplus Objects for Sales Document Categories.

  • Custom Adaption: ITEM_CATEGORY_DETERMINATITEION

 

  • Select Document Category: C (Orders) And Press Enter

     

A BRFplus application template is provided. The application template includes a function template and an expression template.

 

  • Press The Copy Button to Enter the PRFplus Object (PRFplus Application)  

     

     

  • Save Your Configuration
  • Navigate Simplified BRFplus Workbench app and Configure your Business Rules.

     

  • In the following Application you can define the new Business Rules to your Decision Table.
    • Find your application in the repository.
    • Expand the application node, the expression node, and then the decision table node.
    • Choose the decision table to display it.
    • Click Edit

 

 

 

  • Now you can add a row to your decision table and add values in cells as shown below.

     

  • You can now check, save and activate Your Business Rules.
     
  • Recommendation

    To prevent any inconsistencies between your business rules and the existing item category assignments in the Assign Item Categories configuration activity, choose values for the following conditions and the result by using the search help:

    • Sales document type
    • Item category group
    • Item category of higher-level item
    • Item usage
    • Item category

     Results

    The implementation is complete for the sales document category to which the activated BRFplus application, function, and expression are assigned. The flexible item category determination is enabled for sales documents that belong to the sales document category. 

Testing the Configuration

  1. Create Standard Sales Order And Choose the Order Reason defined in your Business Rule.

 

Thanks!

entry.title: File Uplaod in cloud using RAP.
entry.content:
RAP File Uploads 

In the context of SAP's Rapid Application Programming (RAP) model, file uploads enable you to incorporate file handling capabilities into your applications. This empowers users to upload various file types, such as PDFs, Excel spreadsheets, images, and more. 

Uploading Large Object and media such as Excel or Image through your application is a common business requirement and the only way to do it through a RAP application was by extending the application and using UI5 tooling to upload the file. 

With the latest SAP BTP ABAP 2208 release the RAP framework now supports OData streams. It is now possible to enable your RAP application to maintain and handle Large Objects(LOBs).This feature provides end users an option to upload external files of different file formats such as PDF, XLSX, binary file format and other types hence allowing media handling. 

In this Blog we will explore how to upload and handle Large Object such as PDF or Binary files without the need to extend the RAP application in BAS. 

Large objects are modeled by means of the following fields: 

Attachment 

Mime type 

Filename 

The field Attachment contains the LOB itself in a RAWSTRING format and is technically bound to the field Mime type and Filename using semantics annotation. 

Mimetype represents the content type of the attachment uploaded and the values for the fields Mime type and Filename are derived from the field Attachment by the RAP framework based on the maintained CDS annotations. No attachment can exist without its mime type and vice versa. 

For example, when a PDF is uploaded the Mime type field will be derived and populated with ‘APPLICATION/PDF’ 

 

Key Considerations: 

  • RAP Version: The specific approach to file uploads may vary slightly depending on the RAP version you're using. 
  • File Types: The types of files you intend to support will influence the configuration and implementation. 
  • File Size Limits: Consider setting appropriate file size limits to prevent performance issues and potential security risks. 
  • Security: Implement robust security measures to protect uploaded files, including access control and data encryption. 

General Steps: 

  1. Model the File: Define a suitable data type for the file in your RAP business object. Consider using a Large Object (LOB) data type to accommodate large files. 
  2. Create the Upload Service: Expose a service that allows clients to upload files. Implement the necessary logic to handle the uploaded file, such as saving it to a specific location or processing its content. 
  3. Client-Side Implementation: Create a user interface element (e.g., a file input field) to allow users to select files. Use appropriate HTTP methods (e.g., POST) to send the file to the server. Handle the server's response to indicate success or failure of the upload. 

Specific Approaches: 

  • OData Streams:  
  • Leverage OData streams to directly handle file uploads within your RAP application. 
  • This approach is well-suited for modern RAP versions and provides a streamlined way to manage file uploads. 

Business scenario. 

In the Sales and Distribution (SD) module, companies often receive bulk sales orders from customers in the form of Excel or CSV files, especially in industries like wholesale or distribution. Manually entering these orders into SAP is time-consuming and prone to errors.   

Database Tables.

 

 

@EndUserText.label : 'material header data' 

@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE 

@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT 

@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A 

@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED 

define table znp_dt_mara1 { 

 

key client : abap.clnt not null; 

key matnr : znp_de_matnr1 not null; 

mtart : mtart; 

mbrsh : mbrsh; 

@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure : 'znp_t_mara.gewei' 

brgew : brgew; 

gewei : gewei; 

@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure : 'znp_t_mara.gewei' 

mweight : brgew; 

materialstatus : abap.char(10); 

lvorm : lvorm; 

attachement : znp_de_media; 

filename : abap.char(128); 

mimetype : abap.char(128); 

created_by : abp_creation_user; 

created_at : abp_creation_tstmpl; 

last_changed_by : abp_locinst_lastchange_user; 

last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl; 

 

}

 

 

 

Interface view.

 

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK 

@Metadata.allowExtensions: true 

@EndUserText.label: '###GENERATED Core Data Service Entity' 

define root view entity ZR_NP_DT_MARA1 

as select from znp_dt_mara1 

{ 

key matnr as Matnr, 

mtart as Mtart, 

mbrsh as Mbrsh, 

@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'Gewei' 

brgew as Brgew, 

gewei as Gewei, 

@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'Gewei' 

mweight as Mweight, 

materialstatus as Materialstatus, 

lvorm as Lvorm, 

@Semantics.largeObject : { mimeType: 'Mimetype', 

fileName: 'Filename', 

contentDispositionPreference: #ATTACHMENT }  

attachement as Attachement, 

filename as Filename, 

@Semantics.mimeType: true 

mimetype as Mimetype, 

@Semantics.user.createdBy: true 

created_by as CreatedBy, 

@Semantics.systemDateTime.createdAt: true 

created_at as CreatedAt, 

@Semantics.user.localInstanceLastChangedBy: true 

last_changed_by as LastChangedBy, 

@Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true 

last_changed_at as LastChangedAt 

}

 

 

 

Projection views and extension.

 

 

@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@EndUserText.label: '###GENERATED Core Data Service Entity'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
define root view entity ZC_NP_DT_MARA1
  provider contract TRANSACTIONAL_QUERY
  as projection on ZR_NP_DT_MARA1
{
  key Matnr,
  Mtart,
  Mbrsh,
  Brgew,
  @Semantics.unitOfMeasure: true
  Gewei,
  Mweight,
  Materialstatus,
  Lvorm,
  Attachement,
  Filename,
  Mimetype,
  CreatedBy,
  CreatedAt,
  LastChangedBy,
  LastChangedAt
  
}




@Metadata.layer: #CORE
@UI.headerInfo.title.type: #STANDARD
@UI.headerInfo.title.value: 'Matnr'
@UI.headerInfo.description.type: #STANDARD
@UI.headerInfo.description.value: 'Matnr'
annotate view ZC_NP_DT_MARA1 with
{
  .facet: [ {

entry.title: Storage Unit Management with Identification and Pick Points in WM
entry.content:
Storage Unit Management in SAP WM allows for the efficient handling of storage units within a warehouse. A storage unit can be any container or pallet that holds materials.

Identification Points in SAP WM

Identification points are critical for tracking and managing storage units. These points are strategically placed within the warehouse to ensure that each storage unit is correctly identified and recorded.

Pick Points in SAP WM

Pick points are designated areas within the warehouse where materials are collected for order fulfillment. Effective management of pick points can significantly enhance the picking process.

Let's consider an example scenario for ID Point;

ID Point Storage Type: 122 – Identification Point

Main Storage Type: 118 – BC Contact Materials

If material is coming to main destination Storage type, then first it must come to ID Point Storage type and then it must move from ID point storage type to Main Storage type.

1. Storage type Configuration Path:

IMG > Logistics Execution > Warehouse Management > Master Data > Define Storage Type

Select ID Point Storage Type and navigate to details

Stock Removal Strategy is not required for ID point storage type

Select Main Storage Type and navigate to details

 

Maintain Storage type as 118 for Put away and stock Removal operations (with or without storage type Indicator)

2. Storage type Bin search Configuration Path:

IMG > Logistics Execution > Warehouse Management > Strategies > Activate Storage Bin Type Search

Test Case

Step 1: Create a PO and do the GRN

Step 2: Check the stock before doing GRN

Step 3: Create TO using LT06 and transfer the stock using Foreground option.

Step 4: Check stock in LS26, stock updated in ID point Storage Type.

Uploaded stock 50,000 EA (500 BOX)

Total Stock: 50,350 EA

Step 5: Create Transfer order to move the stock from ID point to Main storage type using LT09 Transaction.

Tick Confirm check box and click on Create Transfer order

Once you click on Create TO, Destination Storage Bin details automatically fetched

Step 6: Check the stock in LS26 Transaction

Let's consider an example scenario for Pick Point;

Pick Point Storage Type: 122 – Pick Point Point

Main Storage Type: 118 – BC Contact Materials

Once Transfer confirmed and stock removed from the SU, remaining stock wil automatically shown in 122 storage tye (Pick point), This leftover stock will be moved back to original main store by creating TO using LT09.

Step 1: Check Full stock removal check box in storage type master data screen

Step 2: Check the stock in LS26

In our case 1BAG contains 100 EA.

Total stock 50,000 EA

Step 3: Goods Issue posted for 150 EA using MIGO Transaction

Step 4: Create Transfer order

Step 5: Confirm Transfer order item using LT11 Transaction

If stock removal happens from multiple storage units.

  1. Enter the number of first storage unit that you want to remove completely from storage
  2. Enter the number of second storage units that you want to completely remove from the stock.

Once you have confirmed the last TO item for picking, the system posts the SU with the remaining quantity of 49,850 EA to the pick point (122 Str. type). The SU is therefore at the pick point, both physically and according to the system.

Step 6: Check the stock

Remaining stock will automatically shown in storage type 122 (Pick point str. Type)

To move this stock back to main storage type, create TO in LT09 Transaction

Step 7: Create TO using LT09 Transaction.

As per storage bin type search strategy system will picks the storage bin automatically by clicking the create Transfer Order and confirm check box.

Step 8: Confirm the TO

Step 9: Check the stock in LS26 Transaction

entry.title: Version Management in Purchase Order(PO)
entry.content:
Version Management in SAP allows users to create and manage different versions of a purchase order. Each version captures the state of the PO at a specific point in time, enabling users to track changes and maintain a history of modifications. This is particularly useful in complex procurement processes where multiple changes may occur before finalizing a PO.

Step 1: Activate Version Management

To use version management, it must first be activated in the SAP system. This can be done through the following steps:

  • Go to SPRO.
  • Navigate to Materials Management > Purchasing > Purchase Order > Version Management.
  • Activate version management for the relevant document types.

Step 2: Configure Version Management

Once activated, configure the settings to suit your organization's needs:

  • Define the version number increment.
  • Set up the conditions under which a new version is created (e.g., changes in quantity, price, delivery date).
  • Specify the fields that should trigger version creation.

Step 3: Create and Manage Versions

When changes are made to a PO, a new version is created automatically based on the configured settings. Users can view and manage these versions through the PO history:

  • Access the PO in the SAP system.
  • Navigate to the Version Overview tab to see all versions.
  • Compare different versions to understand the changes made.

Process Steps:

1.Set Up Version Management for External Purchasing Document

SPRO >Materials Management > Purchasing > Version Management > Set Up Version Management for External Purchasing Documents.

Select the document type and navigate to Details option

In control data, check the version active and version 0 OK and in field selection based on your business needs chose whether the fields are optional or mandatory entries.

2. Define Reasons for Change

SPRO > Materials Management > Purchasing > Version Management > Define Reasons for Change

Here you can maintain the different reasons for changinf the purchase order

3. Set Up Change Displays

SPRO > Materials Management > Purchasing > Version Management > Set Up Change Displays

Enter fields for document type and check for version as active

4. Create Purchase Order

Version 0 will be created automatically once after the PO creation

5. Change the PO

Quantity change from 3 to 5

Since we chosen this fields as required fields user must enter the data to save the PO

Once saved new version will be created in the Version tab in PO header

Price changed from 300 EUR to 500 EUR

Version 2 generated for the new changes

entry.title: Ariba Network Invoicing with OCR
entry.content:

Thanks,
Velu
